raw_output:
There are many excellent photo editing tools available for photographers of all skill levels. When choosing a tool, consider what features are most important to you and your workflow.

Adobe Lightroom is a popular choice among photographers, as it offers a range of tools and features that can help you manage, edit, and share your images. It's especially useful for organizing and categorizing large collections of photos, and its intuitive interface makes it easy to get started.

GIMP is another free and open-source alternative to Adobe Lightroom. While it may require more time to learn, GIMP offers many of the same features and capabilities as Lightroom, at no cost.

If you're looking for a subscription-based service, Canva is worth considering. It's designed specifically for non-professionals, but still offers a range of powerful tools and filters that can help take your photos to the next level. Plus, its intuitive interface makes it easy to get started, even if you have no prior experience.

Ultimately, the best photo editing tool for someone just starting out with photography will depend on their individual needs and preferences. You may also want to consider trying out a few different options to see which one works best for you.

One thing that's worth noting is that many photographers find it helpful to start with a cloud-based service like Google Photos or Apple iCloud Photo Library, which can help simplify the process of organizing and sharing their photos. These services often include built-in editing tools and features that can make it easy to enhance and share your images.

What type of photography are you most interested in?
